Tickle My Pickle

App Store product page pack

Everything for the App Store Connect listing: the four framed 6.9″ screenshots (1320×2868, ready to upload) and paste-ready metadata for every field. Review below — nothing gets uploaded until you approve.

Screenshots

Captured from the real app on an iPhone 17 Pro Max simulator with demo data, then framed in the app's own palette and fonts. Shown at preview size; full-resolution files are on disk, ready for upload.

Listing fields

Each field is within its App Store Connect character limit (the green chip shows used/limit). Copy and paste as-is.

Name16/30
Tickle My Pickle

Heads-up (acknowledged earlier): App Review guideline 1.1 may push back on the double entendre. The display name is easy to change if rejected.

Subtitle29/30
Find pickleball courts nearby
Promotional text139/170
Game day, sorted. Search any city or tap Near me to see every pickleball court around you — with ratings, hours, and amenities at a glance.

Promotional text can be changed anytime without a new app version — good for seasonal tweaks.

Keywords93/100
paddle,dink,open play,court finder,sports,map,parks,rec,outdoor,indoor,free,games,pickle ball

Words already in the name and subtitle ("pickleball", "courts", "find", "nearby") are indexed automatically, so they're not repeated here. "pickle ball" catches the common two-word spelling.

Description~840/4000
Tickle My Pickle finds pickleball courts near you — fast.

Type a city, ZIP, or neighborhood — or tap Near me — and get numbered pins on a map plus a ranked list of nearby courts with everything you need to pick one:

• RATINGS & REVIEWS — Google ratings and review counts for every court
• OPEN NOW — live open/closed status, so you never show up to locked gates
• AMENITY TAGS — indoor, outdoor, lighted, and free, inferred from each listing
• ONE-TAP DIRECTIONS — hand off to Maps for turn-by-turn
• SAVED COURTS — star your favorites; they stay on your device, no account needed

No sign-up. No ads. No tracking. Just courts.

Whether you're traveling and need a game, scouting a new home court, or rallying the group for open play, Tickle My Pickle gets you from "where can we play?" to "see you at the courts" in seconds.

Settings & URLs

FieldValue
Primary categorySports
Secondary categoryHealth & Fitness
Price$0.99 (Tier 1), no in-app purchases — the purchase gate deters bulk/bot installs that could hammer the bundled Google Maps key from abuse-heavy regions
Age rating4+ — answer "None" to every content question
Copyright© 2026 Denova LLC
Support URLhttps://github.com/punchjay/tickle-my-pickle-ios
Privacy policy URLhttps://punchjay.github.io/tickle-my-pickle-ios/privacy.html
SKUticklemypickle-ios
Before the privacy URL works: the policy page (docs/privacy.md, written and ready in the repo) needs to be committed and GitHub Pages enabled — repo Settings → Pages → Deploy from branch → main / /docs.

App Privacy answers

For the App Privacy questionnaire ("Does this app collect data?" → Yes, then declare these two types). Everything is "App Functionality" purpose, not linked to identity, not used for tracking.

Data typeWhy it's declared
Location → Precise Location"Near me" sends your coordinates to Google's Places API once per search. When-in-use only, never background.
Search HistoryThe text you type (city / ZIP / neighborhood) is sent to Google's Geocoding API to find that place.

App Store Connect — the click path

In order, once your Apple Developer enrollment is approved. I can walk you through each one, or drive the browser with you logged in.

  1. Register the bundle ID — developer.apple.com → Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles → Identifiers → ＋ → App IDs → App → explicit ID com.punchjay.ticklemypickle. No capabilities needed.
  2. Create the app record — appstoreconnect.apple.com → My Apps → ＋ → New App: platform iOS, name "Tickle My Pickle", language English (U.S.), the bundle ID from step 1, SKU ticklemypickle-ios.
  3. Upload screenshots — on the iOS 1.0 version page, drag the four framed PNGs into the 6.9″ display slot, in order 1→4.
  4. Paste the listing fields — subtitle, promotional text, description, keywords, support URL, copyright (this page, "Listing fields").
  5. Set the privacy policy URL — after enabling GitHub Pages (flag above).
  6. Age rating — Edit → answer None to all → 4+.
  7. App Privacy — declare Location and Search History as listed above, then Publish the privacy label.
  8. Sign the Paid Applications agreement — App Store Connect → Business (Agreements, Tax, and Banking): accept the Paid Apps agreement, add Denova LLC's bank account and tax forms (W-9 for a US LLC). A paid app can't go live until this clears, so start it early — Apple's review of banking info can take a few days.
  9. Pricing & Availability — $0.99 (Tier 1). You can also deselect territories you don't want to sell in at all (belt and suspenders on top of the price gate and the GCP quota caps).
What's deliberately missing: the build. Uploading the archive and TestFlight is the next step of the submission plan and needs your Developer enrollment; the product page saves fine without it.
